exemplar.title:
How does Toni Morrison use the motif of haunting in her characters to 
explore the lasting psychological trauma of slavery in Beloved?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How does Toni Morrison use the motif of haunting in her characters to 
explore the lasting psychological trauma of slavery in Beloved?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Topic and purpose are communicated accurately throughout, with a clear title and focused headings linking back to the research question.
The research question is clearly stated, tightly focused on âhauntingâ in Beloved, and consistently referenced in each section.
Methodology draws on a reasonable range of primary and secondary sources plus interviews, demonstrating informed selection.
weaknesses: Some methodological details are omitted (e.g., interview protocol, dates, participant roles), weakening transparency.
Minor digressions into broader historical commentary interrupt focus precision.
Occasional repetitive phrasing in describing the essayâs purpose reduces stylistic concision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed the work solidly in the 5â6 band for communication and RQ focus (both band-5) and in the 3â4 band for methodology (band-4). The clear framing and sustained focus justify a high mark, but methodological omissions prevent the top of the band. A score of 5/6 best reflects these strengths and limitations.

strengths: Demonstrates good textual knowledge of key scenes and integrates Cathy Caruthâs trauma theory effectively.
Uses subject-specific terminology (e.g., âmagical realismâ, ânon-linear narrativeâ, âuncannyâ) mostly accurately.
Shows coherent understanding of how form and theme interact in Belovedâs narrative structure.
weaknesses: Coverage of secondary material is sometimes superficial (e.g., oral tradition references without depth).
Occasional imprecision in terminology (misnaming Denver as âDeniseâ) and minor grammatical lapses.
Bibliography entries lack full publication details, reducing source credibility.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand feedback placed knowledge of topic at band-4 and terminology use around mid band-3 to 4. While the student shows good comprehension and terminology command, errors and superficial coverage limit excellence. A 4/6 accurately balances these factors.

strengths: Research draws on relevant primary text and recognized theorists, showing consistent link to the research question.
Analysis moves beyond descriptionâe.g., the âtobacco tinâ metaphor is examined incisively with solid textual support.
Demonstrates strong understanding of how narrative form (nonlinear structure) reinforces reader empathy and theme.
weaknesses: Relies at times on non-scholarly sources (e.g., Britannica entry on Freud), limiting academic depth.
Critical evaluation is uneven: counter-arguments and alternative readings (e.g., Denverâs agency) are underdeveloped.
The conclusion tends to summarize rather than synthesize broader implications, reducing evaluative impact.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fell within the 4â6 band (research quality 5, analysis 5, evaluation 4). The workâs consistently relevant research and solid analytical insights justify raising to the top of this band, but uneven critical evaluation prevents a band-7â9 score. A score of 6/12 best reflects these considerations.

strengths: Includes all required components (title page with word count, table of contents, section headings, page numbers).
Structure is logical and aids navigation through themed chapters.
Layout is generally clear and readable, supporting the argumentâs flow.
weaknesses: Citation style is inconsistent (mixed indentation, stray LaTeX code) and bibliography formatting is uneven.
Some paragraphs are overly long and complex, obscuring key points.
Minor pagination and formatting inconsistencies (e.g., TOC alignment, indentation) detract from professionalism.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation strands were in the 3â4 band (structure 4, layout 3). The essay meets structural requirements and readability standards, but inconsistencies in citation style and formatting errors prevent a flawless presentation. A top-band score of 4/4 accurately reflects its strengths and areas for improvement.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay cites primary text plus seven secondary sources and describes interviews with a psychologist, **showing a reasonable but not extensive range of methods.** Source selection is relevant but the Works Cited contains inconsistencies (mixed citation styles, limited publishing details) and the âinterviewâ method is mentioned but not documented. These shortcomings suggest a mostly complete, yet not fully rigorous, methodologyâfitting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

Page numbers, word count and headings are present, and overall layout is readable. **Citation style is inconsistent** (bulleted list in Works Cited, varied indentation) and there is stray LaTeX code (â$\overline{7}$â, â$d\rho$â, page markers). These issues lower professionalism but do not negate correct application of basic EE formatting, warranting a low-mid score within the 3-4 band.

The research materialâprimary novel, recognised theorists (Caruth, Freud, LaCapra, Gordon)âis **generally relevant and linked to the RQ.** The breadth is moderate, not extensive, and some sources (Britannica entry on Freud) are quite introductory, preventing a higher band. Nevertheless, overall research supports the exploration with reasonable depth, aligning with the midpoint of the 4-6 band.

**The student states the topic explicitly in the title and reiterates it in the opening paragraph,** linking haunting to psychological and historical trauma in Beloved. Purpose is reiterated throughout (âthis essay argues thatâ¦â) and the focus on trauma remains visible in each major section heading, demonstrating sustained clarity. Minor digressions and some repetitive phrasing slightly weaken precision, so the top of the band is not fully met, but overall communication is accurate and purposeful.

The argument is coherent, progressing through Sethe, Denver and Beloved as symbolic strands, and there is some evaluation of theoretical perspectives (Freud vs Caruth). **However, critical weighing of alternative readings is limited and counter-arguments are rare.** Discussion remains more explanatory than fully evaluative, fitting the lower half of the 4-6 band.

**Analysis moves beyond description,** connecting narrative structure to trauma theory and providing textual evidence. Conclusions (e.g., healing through community) are mostly supported, though at times assertions are repetitive and not fully interrogated. Depth is solid but not consistently sophisticated, aligning with a strong mid-band score.

The essay contains all required componentsâtitle page with word count, abstract-style introduction, clear section headings, conclusion and Works Cited. **Logical sequencing through themed chapters and a functional table of contents** demonstrate an appropriate structure. Minor pagination errors and some over-long paragraphs prevent flawless execution, so the work sits comfortably in the 3-4 band.

**Textual knowledge is sound:** key scenes (â124 was spitefulâ, Paul Dâs âtobacco tinâ) are quoted accurately and linked to theory (Caruth, Freud). Secondary material is used, but coverage is selective and sometimes superficial (e.g., brief references to âAfrican-American oral traditionâ without depth). This demonstrates good rather than excellent knowledge, meriting the 3-4 range.

Subject terminologyââmagical realismâ, ânon-linear narrativeâ, ârepressionâ, âuncannyââis used frequently and usually correctly, **showing mostly accurate command.** However, there are moments of imprecision (misnaming Denver as âDeniseâ, conflating âtheory of ghostsâ with Caruthâs ideas) and occasional grammatical lapses. This places the work in the middle of the 3-4 band.

The research questionââHow does Toni Morrison use the motif of hauntingâ¦?ââis **clearly framed, focused on one novel and one literary device, and is consistently referenced** in analysis sections. Each body chapter explicitly addresses an aspect of haunting, indicating a tight link between RQ and discussion. Occasional drift into broad historical comments prevents a flawless connection, placing the work solidly but not maximally within the 5-6 band.

annotations.comment: The word count is provided and falls just under the 4000-word limit, satisfying the formatting requirement for transparency.
The Table of Contents correctly lists all major sections with corresponding page numbers, supporting clear navigation through the essayâs structure.
Consider indenting subentries and aligning page numbers to the right margin in the TOC to enhance readability and professional presentation.
The introduction situates the research in its literary and historical context and states a clear thesis about fragmented structure and trauma.
The title precisely conveys the investigationâs focus on trauma and haunting in Beloved, meeting the formatting requirement for a clear, descriptive title.
The research question is succinctly worded and effectively frames the analysis scope by focusing on haunting as a motif for psychological trauma.
The opening paragraph contains very long, multi-clause sentences that may obscure key points; consider splitting them for greater clarity.
To improve methodological transparency, add a brief outline at the end of the introduction summarizing the essayâs structure and research steps.
The methodology section mentions interviews with a psychologist but omits details such as dates or participant roles, weakening method credibility.
Provide a concise description of the interview protocol (e.g., number of interviews, questions asked) to strengthen methodological rigor.
Relying primarily on a Britannica entry for Freudâs theory limits scholarly depth; consider citing Freudâs original texts or peer-reviewed journal articles.
The integration of Cathy Caruthâs trauma theory shows strong use of critical sources and deepens the essayâs theoretical basis.
Several pronoun shifts between Freud and Caruth discussions create potential confusion; specify the theorist in each sentence to maintain clarity.
The heading for the section on nonlinear narrative effectively signals a transition and reinforces the essayâs focus.
The metaphor of Paul Dâs âtobacco tinâ is analyzed incisively with strong textual evidence, demonstrating solid analytical depth.
In-text citations vary (e.g., "Morrison 3" vs "Morrison 6"); standardize to one citation format (MLA or APA) to improve consistency.
Consider adding subheadings within this dense analysis to guide the reader through your argumentâs progression.
A stray LaTeX command (â\overline{7}â) appears in the footer, which detracts from the essayâs professionalism and should be removed.
The text refers to the character as âDeniseâ instead of Denver in one instance; ensure character names are checked for accuracy throughout.
The discussion of fragmented stream-of-consciousness sections accurately captures how style reflects traumaâs disorientation.
The linkage between nonlinear narrative and reader empathy displays a strong understanding of how form reinforces theme.
To bolster claims about oral tradition, incorporate a secondary source on African-American storytelling practices and cite specific examples.
The analysis of Denverâs inherited trauma lacks engagement with alternative readings, such as her exercise of agency versus victimhood.
Include citations of scenes where Denver actively seeks help to demonstrate her agency and enrich your thematic argument.
The conclusion effectively reaffirms the research question but largely summarizes earlier points instead of synthesizing broader implications.
The conclusion does link findings back to the research question and demonstrates a coherent closure to the analysis.
Works Cited entries use mixed indentation and formatting styles, detracting from professionalism; standardize indentation and font consistently.
Several bibliography entries lack full publication details (e.g., DOI, publisher location), reducing source credibility; provide complete citations.
Alphabetize the Works Cited entries and apply consistent list formatting to adhere to MLA/APA conventions and improve usability.
